name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 54 Tuesday AM. UREA GRANULAR FOB ME $702/mt SPOT / $830/mt FORWARD (+81.7% YTD, +69.6% YoY) — STRUCTURAL SCARCITY REASSERTED; CF INDUSTRIES +2.9% MON REVERSED FRIDAY'S -9.6%. Trading Economics / CME levels; pre-war $400-490/mt. Qatar production halt on urea CONFIRMED held (QatarEnergy); Saudi/Bahrain/UAE nitrogen supply constrained. Monday drivers (~2.4% of CF's +2.9%): structural urea scarcity + UBS PT raised to $140 from $97 ('potential for further upside in nitrogen prices'). Nutrien +0.8% (potash-heavier, less Qatar-exposed); the CF/Nutrien split IS the genuine signal. Linde $498.15 (+1.2%) industrial gas proxy firm. Agri complex bid Mon 2nd-order: Wheat +2.5%, Corn +2.4%, Sugar +2.4%, Palm Oil +2.0% — fertilizer-input pass-through + planting-reduction fears. CF Monday thesis weakened by TTF retrace to €40.56 (removed fresh input-cost-shock catalyst) — but structural urea levels already well above trigger-point. India cleanest transmission: 25-30% urea import dependency, food-CPI with 1-2 quarter lag; RBI FY27 4.6% CPI may under-weight fertilizer-pass-through. LOW reversibility: Qatar production restart takes weeks post-ceasefire; Q2 planting decisions locked at crisis-adjusted input costs. Profercy World Nitrogen Index 352.95 = May 2022 high structural.

current.price: Urea Granular FOB ME $702/mt spot / $830/mt forward; CF Industries $115.94; Nutrien $71.17; Linde $498.15

price_snapshots.trigger: QatarEnergy shut world's largest urea plant after Ras Laffan damage
Triple disruption compounding: QAFCO offline + Hormuz blocking + India feedstock cuts
price_snapshots.delta_context: More than doubled from $350/ton (late 2025). CF Industries +24.1% MoM.

price_snapshots.trigger: Most elevated crisis indicator -- +68% from pre-crisis $500, exceeding Brent's +49%
price_snapshots.delta_context: 5 of 8 major fertilizers +5% weekly. NOT responding to ceasefire.

signals.content: QatarEnergy shut world's largest urea plant after Ras Laffan damage. Urea export prices surged ~40% from under $500 to over $700/mt almost immediately.
PM Modi in Lok Sabha assured farmers of fertilizer availability despite global price spikes. Government absorbing some cost through subsidies.

QatarEnergy expanded production suspension beyond LNG to include entire downstream: urea, methanol, polymers, and aluminum. QAFCO's 5.6 Mt/y Mesaieed urea plant -- world's largest single-site urea facility -- offline since March 4. Gulf producers account for 43% of all seaborne urea exports, now largely offline. US retail urea price at $674/ton (second week of March), up 12% MoM. New Orleans barge prices jumped from $475 to $520-550/ton in single week. CRU Group warns disruptions 'could be catastrophic' if prolonged. WEF published analysis stating crisis 'demonstrates the fragility of global food supply.' India's situation: government claims record pre-Kharif stocks (59-62 lakh tonnes urea) but LNG supply cuts from Qatar forced 30% feedstock reduction to domestic fertilizer plants -- three urea plants reduced output. India sources 40%+ of urea from Middle East. Government quietly approached China for urea supply (BigStory Network) while publicly denying shortage. Imported urea subsidies expected to increase 52%+.

Urea at ~$640/mt (+12% MoM). India approaching China for emergency urea supply while publicly denying shortage. Domestic fertilizer plants at 70% capacity due to LNG feedstock cuts from Qatar disruption. European TTF surge (+70% in March to EUR54.52/MWh) directly increases fertilizer production costs globally -- natural gas is primary feedstock for nitrogen-based fertilizers. Connected to food security cascade for India: Kharif 2026 planting season at risk if supply doesn't normalize.

COMPREHENSIVE UPDATE: Global urea prices surged from $350/ton (late 2025) to over $800/ton in US Gulf and $720+/ton globally -- more than doubled in one month. Three layers of disruption compounding: (1) QAFCO offline since March 4 (14% of global urea, 5-year repair timeline), (2) Hormuz blocking 43% of seaborne urea exports, (3) India's domestic production hit by LNG feedstock cut -- Petronet LNG invoked force majeure March 3, gas supply at ~70% of requirement, IFFCO and other plants shut or reduced output. India's stocks at 61.14 LMT (vs 55.22 last year) -- looks adequate but drawdown rate exceeds replenishment. Government secured 23% more spot gas (32->39.31 MMSCMD) but at expensive TTF-linked prices. FY27 fertilizer subsidy budget: Rs1.70 lakh crore (DOWN from Rs1.86 lakh crore) -- budgeted LESS while urea prices more than doubled. Import subsidy allocation Rs32,000 crore vs Rs52,000 crore last year. Either mid-year revision (fiscal deficit widens) or farmers bear cost. India diversifying to China, Russia, Morocco, Australia -- 1.3M tons ordered but 'some may not arrive on time' (Bloomberg). Critical Kharif timeline: sowing June-July, 65% of annual urea used in Kharif. If stocks deplete by May, sowing area drops 10-15%. CF Industries surging as US nitrogen beneficiary. (Sources: Bloomberg, CNBC, ChemAnalyst, IMARC, Argus Media, Business Standard, CRU Group)

CF Industries -5.6% on de-escalation trade + Mizuho downgrade to Neutral ('nitrogen price surge is temporary, expects decline once conflict ends'). Market pricing: if war ends, QAFCO (14% global urea) potentially resumes -> urea crash from $800/mt. BUT Hormuz decoupling thesis means even if war ends, QAFCO may not resume immediately if Hormuz stays under Iranian selective control. Urea at ~$800/mt US Gulf. India urea shortfall building: ~2 million tonnes by August, with 60%+ of imports and 80% of ammonia/sulfur from Gulf. India stocks 61 LMT but drawdown exceeds replenishment. (Sources: Mizuho, Yahoo Finance, CNBC, Business Standard)

Urea remains at ~$750/mt (FOB ME). QAFCO offline since March 4 (5.6M mt/year, 14% global). Hormuz blocking 43% seaborne urea exports. CF Industries +24.1% MoM -- the market's verdict on disruption duration. India Kharif sowing June-July: 65% of annual urea used in Kharif. India stocks at 61 LMT but drawdown exceeds replenishment. FY27 subsidy budget Rs1.70L cr (DOWN from Rs1.86L cr) -- fiscal math broken at current prices. Diversification to China/Russia/Morocco underway -- 1.3M tons ordered. (Sources: ChemAnalyst, IMARC, Business Standard)

Urea FOB Middle East at $750/mt -- 52-week high, up from $400-490 pre-war. QAFCO 5.6MT/yr shut. China reportedly banned urea exports until August (markets.md source) -- this removes the alternative supply India was quietly approaching after Gulf disruptions. India faces 2M tonne shortfall by August, with 60%+ of imports sourced from Gulf. CF Industries +24.1% monthly ($129.97) -- direct equity proxy for disruption duration. Nutrien +3.3% monthly ($75.47). The China export ban, if confirmed, is the critical new variable: India's diversification strategy (1.3M tons ordered from China/Russia/Morocco/Australia) now has its largest single source blocked. OPEC+ 206K bpd increase confirms supply-side cannot rescue energy costs, which feeds through to fertilizer production costs via natural gas feedstock. Kharif timeline unchanged: sowing June-July, 65% of annual urea consumed in Kharif season. (Sources: ChemAnalyst, Trading Economics, Epoch Times, markets.md)

Urea Egypt FOB at ~$700/mt (from $400-490 pre-war, +43-75%). US Gulf urea at $800/mt -- level last seen during 2022 Russia-Ukraine spike. ~30% of globally traded urea supply offline (Anadolu Agency, Carnegie, WEF). QatarEnergy halted one of world's biggest urea plants. CF Industries +69.9% 3M ($77.08 -> $130.98) as largest US nitrogen producer = direct substitute supplier. Nutrien +29.4% 3M. Northern Hemisphere spring planting season at risk -- fertilizer has NO strategic reserve and NO quick substitute. If crisis extends through May, 2026 planting season takes permanent yield damage. European gas costs (TTF EUR49.95) directly feed fertilizer production costs via Haber-Bosch process. BASF +10.4% 1M as European chemical giant benefits from Gulf competitor disruption. (Sources: Al Fassel News, CNBC, AgWeb, WEF, yfinance)

CF Industries +0.8% 1D / +18.2% 1M / +69.9% 3M to $130.98. Urea FOB ME spot $750/mt at 52-week highs. QAFCO (Qatar) offline; China not exporting until August; India shortfall building. Deadline week premium intact. Nutrien $75.81 +0.5% 1D, +29.4% 3M. The fertilizer complex is pricing a sustained Gulf petchem disruption -- South Pars #2 strike + ~85% of Iranian petchem offline reinforces this trajectory. Indian agriculture exposure significant: kharif planning is now explicitly factoring in urea shortage scenarios.

EVENING UPDATE: Urea $701/t spot, $750/t FOB ME futures (+2.0% 1D, +20% 1M, +81% YoY). 52-week high. Supply 'incredibly tight' -- Qatar/Iran gas field damage. DAP at ~$851/t near historic highs. CF Industries +0.8% 1D, +18.2% 1M, +69.9% 3M. Nutrien +0.5%. No strategic reserve, no quick substitute. Planting season at peak risk. India urea shortfall ~2M tonnes by August, 60%+ imports from Gulf. (Sources: TradingEconomics, CME, AgWeb)

QAFCO STILL OFFLINE -- UREA RECOVERY IN MONTHS NOT DAYS DESPITE CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. Urea $680/t spot NOLA, $750/t FOB Middle East. QAFCO Qatar (the largest single urea production site in the world) remains shut down. Trading slowed pending peace clarity per ICIS/World Fertilizer. India's IFFCO has begun scaling back domestic urea on imported LNG feedstock cost. India 40% reliant on Qatar for urea plant gas. Fertilizer supply restoration takes MONTHS not days even if the ceasefire holds -- physical plant restart, feedstock supply chain rebuild, shipping reroute all sequential. CF Industries should remain bid even as agricultural commodity prices fall (wheat -2.9%, palm oil -3.5%, sugar -2.6% today on ceasefire) because the input-side cost structure is permanently elevated. India's tentative G2G talks with China for urea supply continue.

Ceasefire triggered fertilizer war premium unwinding: CF Industries -5.7% to $126.16, Nutrien -2.6% to $74.88. Ceasefire -> Qatar fertilizer supply normalization hopes -> urea war premium deflating. BUT: QAFCO (world's largest single-site urea plant, 5.6Mt/yr) still offline -- physical plant restart takes months regardless of ceasefire. ~85% of CF's move is crisis-linked; if ceasefire collapses, fertilizer stocks snap back. Urea at $693.50/mt NOLA, +14% YoY, +26.2% surge in March per FinancialContent. Physical disruption persists despite financial market hope. (Source: yfinance, Barchart, TradingEconomics, FinancialContent)

Urea at $693/mt US Gulf (elevated, +39% from pre-crisis $500). CF Industries +1.6%, Nutrien +1.3% -- fertilizer stocks STRONG despite broader war-trade unwinding (energy -0.7%, defense -0.9%, tankers -1.4%). The war trade has NOT unwound for fertilizer: Hormuz remains functionally closed and Qatar Ras Laffan (major fertilizer hub via QAFCO) production still disrupted. Even ceasefire hasn't normalized fertilizer supply chains. CF Industries +51% 3M -- largest crisis-beneficiary among tracked bellwether stocks. Natural gas feedstock costs still elevated due to global LNG disruption even though HH natgas at $2.65. India Kharif sowing risk persists -- 65% of annual urea consumed in Kharif season (June-July).

UREA AT $838/mt -- MOST ELEVATED CRISIS INDICATOR vs baseline (+68% from pre-crisis $500, exceeding Brent's +49%) [CONFIRMED, DTN/Argus/FinancialContent]. Five of eight major fertilizers showed 5%+ weekly increases. Fertilizer prices have NOT responded to the ceasefire AT ALL -- Hormuz remains closed and Qatar's fertilizer infrastructure is offline. This is the most honest real-economy signal about whether the ceasefire is translating into supply chain normalization. IT IS NOT. The physical world has not changed -- only futures markets and headlines have. CF Industries +51% 3M (largest crisis beneficiary stock). If India March CPI (Monday release) shows food price acceleration, the fertilizer->india food CPI cascade edge ACTIVATES. (Sources: DTN, Argus, FinancialContent)

Urea at $693.50 futures / $838+ spot -- MOST ELEVATED crisis indicator at +68% from pre-crisis $500, exceeding Brent's +49%. NOT responding to ceasefire: Hormuz closed to bulk carriers, QAFCO offline, natural gas feedstock elevated. 5 of 8 major fertilizers +5% weekly. Spring planting pressure intensifying globally. CF Industries +51% 3M. India Kharif sowing at risk if supply doesn't normalize by May.

CRISIS PREMIUM DEFLATING IN EQUITIES, NOT IN PHYSICAL COMMODITY. CF Industries -10.5% 1M ($121.68), Nutrien -10.7% 1M ($74.38) -- monthly declines despite urea remaining at $650-800/mt (still +30-68% above pre-crisis). The equity decline is a POSITIONING UNWIND driven by: (1) ceasefire April 8 triggered aggressive crisis-premium selling in commodity equities, (2) CF/Nutrien had +40-44% 3M runs creating crowded longs, (3) profit-taking + rotation into tech/semis (SMH +14.2% 1M), (4) Henry Hub nat gas -16.6% 1M = US domestic fertilizer input costs falling. The monthly decline is NOT a fundamental change -- urea at $650+/mt remains highly profitable for CF/Nutrien. If ceasefire collapses and Qatar fertilizer supply remains disrupted, these stocks re-rate sharply higher. The 3M performance is still massive (+43.8% CF, +13.2% Nutrien). Physical urea NOT responding to deal hopes -- Hormuz still closed to bulk carriers, QAFCO still offline. (Sources: markets.md, yfinance, CBOT/FinancialContent)

Crisis premium DEFLATING in equities: CF Industries -2.2%, Nutrien -10.5% monthly. De-escalation = Hormuz reopening = Middle East fertilizer supply (Qatar, Saudi, UAE, Bahrain = 20% of global trade) resumes. CF had been up 63% YTD on war premium; now giving back. BUT physical urea STILL at $650+/mt — equity market AHEAD of physical market pricing de-escalation that hasn't occurred. 2.3M tonnes production lost. If talks fail, equity snap-back. (Sources: yfinance, Argus/CRU)

EVENING UPDATE: CF Industries $118.96 (-2.2% 1D, -8.2% 1M), Nutrien $73.62 (-1.0% 1D, -10.5% 1M). Market pricing normalization that HASN'T HAPPENED in physical commodity. CRU says 30% of exportable fertilizer suppliers STILL unavailable — QAFCO offline, Hormuz blocking 43% of seaborne urea exports, China export ban through August. Physical urea at $650+/mt (spot to $800) remains 30-68% above pre-crisis. Equity decline is positioning unwind: crowded longs from +40-44% 3M runs taking profits + rotation into tech/semis. If talks fail and Hormuz stays blocked, CF/Nutrien snap-back +5-8%. The equities-physical divergence mirrors the Brent three-layer disconnection. (Sources: yfinance, CRU, Argus)

Day 50. CF INDUSTRIES +3.2% COUNTER-DIRECTIONAL WHILE BRENT CRASHED -6.5% — SUPPLY DISRUPTION PERSISTS BEYOND DEAL [CONFIRMED price action]. Urea at $713/st NOLA (+16.8% 1M, +50% from pre-crisis). Hormuz blockade chokes ~50% seaborne urea. CF benefits: North American producer, domestic natgas feedstock, NOT Hormuz-dependent. UBS raised PT to $140 (from $97). Spring planting = inelastic demand. Even if war ends, rebuilding fertilizer logistics takes months. Qatar downstream halted. 'Elevated through 2027' per Brownfield — persistent property, not shock signal. The CF counter-directional move is the purest 'physical market' signal in equities. (Sources: yfinance, Argus, UBS, TradingEconomics)

EVENING UPDATE: CF DIVERGENCE FROM BRENT IS LARGEST OF THE WAR [CONFIRMED Thursday close — US markets closed Good Friday Apr 17]. CF Industries closed Thursday $124.71 (+3.2%) on the same day Brent crashed intraday -6.5% to $92.92 and Heating Oil -8.7%. UBS raised CF PT to $140 from $97 (+44%). Nutrien $74.53 (+1.6%), Linde $499.22 (+0.3%), BASF structural +18% 1M — the fertilizer/agri-input complex holding firm while war-premium plays (Defense ITA -2.1%, Frontline -1.0%) sold off. Second-order signal: Corn +2.5% Friday — spring planting cost-push from urea/fertilizer (~40% crisis-attributable, 60% USDA acreage/weather dynamics). The fertilizer-to-grains cascade is real but partial; crisis-side does NOT reverse on a ceasefire — fertilizer chains take MONTHS to reset. Investor implication: fertilizer/agri-input names (CF, NTR, MOS, Yara) are the residual 'physical disruption' trade if headline ceasefire materializes but physical chains stay broken — will OUTPERFORM defense, energy, and shipping in a 'Hormuz reopens slowly' scenario. (Sources: yfinance CF/NTR/LIN Thursday closes CONFIRMED, UBS research via market wire, CRU urea spot)

CF INDUSTRIES CRASHED -9.6% TO $112.68 ON FRIDAY — COUNTER-DIRECTIONAL THESIS FAILED [CONFIRMED]. CORRECTS evening brief's Thursday +3.2% framing — Friday April 17 (same session the evening brief was written in) saw CF crash -9.65% to $112.68; larger outlets cite -10.4% intraday. $12 round-trip from thesis-confirming peak to below-pre-escalation price. Catalyst: Araghchi's 'Hormuz completely open' declaration. Urea NOLA FOB -18% WoW to ~$640/st (from $780 peak). Nutrien -5.2% to $70.62; Cheniere -4.4% to $251.07; Linde -1.4% to $492.23. The full nitrogen/fertilizer/LNG supply-disruption complex repriced lower on the same catalyst that crashed Brent -7.6%. This refutes the evening brief's thesis that the fertilizer-urea → strait-of-hormuz edge is independent of deal pricing. The physical supply-chain constraint IS real (repair IS months, not days), BUT the market prices the ANNOUNCEMENT as the structural fix long before repair completes. What survived from the thesis: Frontline +5.6% counter-directional — the shipping-tankers → strait-of-hormuz edge DID hold as deal-rhetoric-orthogonal. Physical sense: tanker rates are set in real spot market with limited fleet supply; fertilizer futures are set by expectations of supply arriving months out. GRAPH ENGINEER NOTE: fertilizer-urea → strait-of-hormuz edge type should note 'market-pricing co-moves with deal rhetoric even though physical repair is months-out.' Weight may remain high on physical fundamentals; signal behavior is NOT independent of deal pricing. Quiver Quant: 'CF Industries shares slide as geopolitical risk premium fades.' REVERSIBILITY: if Monday Islamabad collapses, CF recovers most of -9.65% within 2-5 sessions; if delivery, CF stays $110-115 and urea drifts to $580-620. Asymmetry: downside small (deal upside priced), upside large (Iranian reciprocal measures re-closure → $130+ peak). (Sources: yfinance CF/LNG/FRO/NTR/LIN, Quiver Quant, Stocktwits/Investing.com for urea, Bloomberg 'Fertilizer Prices Drop as Hormuz Opening Brings Farmers Relief')

Day 52 Sunday. CLEANEST MONDAY BOUNCE SETUP IN THE COMPLEX. Urea FOB $713/T April 16 (-1% 1D, +16.8% 1M, +75% YoY); Profercy World Nitrogen Index 352.95 — highest since May 2022. Qatar/Saudi/Bahrain/UAE nitrogen essentially frozen; one-third of seaborne urea traverses Hormuz. Friday CF -9.6% to $112.68 was Araghchi deal-optimism reversal; that catalyst is now INVALIDATED by three Sunday developments: (1) SNSC formally ratified IRGC Hormuz closure, re-imposed $1/bbl loaded-tanker fees — transit friction goes UP, not down; (2) Ras Laffan partial restart: North site within 1 month, South late summer, full recovery 3-5 years per QatarEnergy CEO Al-Kaabi; (3) MEA summons reinforces demand-side disruption. Monday Monday re-pricing: base CF +5 to +8% to $118-122; upside CF +8-12% to $122-126 if Monday Brent opens $96+. Frontline analog: $37.13 Friday should hold $37+ Monday. The market-pricing co-movement with deal rhetoric exposed Friday does not reverse the physical truth that rhetorical Hormuz opening does NOT reboot frozen nitrogen flows — those require 6-18 months post-resolution. 'Elevated through 2027' thesis preserved on fundamentals even if equity signal behavior is NOT independent of deal pricing. (Sources: TradingEconomics urea, Profercy World Nitrogen Index, markets.md C4, intel.md A1/B2)

EVENING CONFIRMATION: CF MONDAY GAP-UP SETUP — ARAGHCHI SUNDAY POSTS DO NOT REBOOT NITROGEN [CONFIRMED]. CF Industries Friday -9.6% on pure deal-optimism error. Urea (Yuzhny FOB) $713/T (+16.8% 1M, +75% YoY); Profercy World Nitrogen Index 352.95 (highest since May 2022). 20% of seaborne ammonia + one-third of seaborne urea via Hormuz remain priced-in as risk. Sunday SNSC ratification + Araghchi's Sunday X posts framing chokepoint as insurance-market problem do NOT reboot frozen Qatar/UAE/Saudi/Bahrain nitrogen exports — those require 6-18 months even post-resolution. Expected Monday: CF +5-8% gap-up base ($118-122); +8-12% upside if Brent $100+ ($122-126); structural floor on CF $105 even in full-resolution case (Ras Laffan recovery 3-5 years). Nutrien +3-5% Monday on nitrogen-complex sympathy. If TTF >EUR45/MWh Monday, European industrial gas cost = key nitrogen input cost re-rates further.

CF INDUSTRIES MONDAY GAP-UP +5-8% EXPECTED; NUTRIEN +3-5% ON SNSC + TTF +11% + UREA RE-BID [REPORTED]. Friday CF -9.6% / Nutrien -5.2% was pure deal-optimism mispricing. Urea FOB ~$713/T April 16 (+16.8% 1M, +75% YoY); Profercy World Nitrogen Index 352.95 — highest since May 2022. Monday TTF +11% intraday confirms European nitrogen input-cost re-escalation (ammonia is natural-gas-heavy production → TTF spike propagates to European nitrogen producers via CF European exposure). Structural floor CF ~$105 even in full-resolution case (Ras Laffan damaged trains 3-5 yr recovery). Middle East granular urea FOB ~$410s/t (Argus post-Qatar-disruption Egypt/Algeria benchmark); global spot $702.25/T +15% 1M.

UREA GRANULAR FOB ME $702/mt SPOT / $830/mt FORWARD (+81.7% YTD, +69.6% YoY); CF INDUSTRIES +2.9% MON REVERSED FRIDAY'S -9.6% [CONFIRMED MARKET DATA; STRUCTURAL SCARCITY REASSERTED]. Trading Economics / CME data; pre-war level $400-490/mt. Reversal dynamics: Friday -9.6% priced ceasefire normalization (flawed thesis given structural Qatar/GCC scarcity); weekend *Touska* + SNSC data reaffirmed Qatar-chain disruption; Monday correctly re-rated structural tightness. Primary chain (crisis ~2.4% of 2.9%): Qatar urea production halt CONFIRMED held → Saudi/Bahrain/UAE nitrogen supply constrained by conflict → India/Brazil import inventories below historical averages → UBS PT raised to $140 from $97 ('potential for further upside in nitrogen prices'). CF/Nutrien split IS the genuine signal: CF pure-play nitrogen (Qatar-correlated), Nutrien potash-heavier (less Qatar-exposed). TTF retrace to €40.56 removed near-term input-cost-shock catalyst but structural Qatar scarcity persists. Agri complex Monday: Wheat +2.5%, Corn +2.4%, Sugar +2.4%, Palm Oil +2.0% = 2nd-order fertilizer transmission (nitrogen-intensive crops bid specifically). India transmission: 25-30% urea import dependency, food-CPI with 1-2 quarter lag; RBI FY27 4.6% CPI may under-weight this. LOW reversibility: Qatar restart takes weeks post-ceasefire; Q2 planting decisions locked at crisis-adjusted input costs. New edge proposed: `fertilizer-urea → india` (directness 2, initial weight 3.5).

CF INDUSTRIES +4.6% TO $121.31 DESPITE CRUDE CRASH — STRUCTURAL NITROGEN SCARCITY DIVERGING FROM CRUDE [CONFIRMED]. Nutrien +2.1% at $72.67 confirms sector bid. CF is clearest example of structural crisis exposure without daily crisis correlation — function of Qatar's base-rate disruption (Ras Laffan unresolved) + spring planting demand + UBS PT $140. Urea FOB ME still at $702/mt (April 17 print, no Wed update). If this were new crisis signal, spot urea would spike too — it isn't. Move is US-domestic fertilizer supply/demand balance. Structural thesis intact.

edges.context: Natural gas is primary input for nitrogen-based fertilizers. Henry Hub -16.6% 1M = US domestic fertilizer input costs falling. But global LNG (TTF) still elevated. US producers (CF, Nutrien) benefit from cheap domestic gas + high urea prices = margin expansion.

Day 50 evening. QAFCO Mesaieed (world's largest single-site urea plant, 5.6 Mt/y) remains offline. Ras Laffan Trains 4 & 6 = 12.8 Mtpa / 17% global LNG offline; August earliest partial restart, 3-5 years full restoration. Ceasefire does not change physical restart timeline. CF +3.2% counter-directional Thursday = equity confirmation of 'elevated through 2027' thesis.

India imports 40%+ of urea from Middle East. Domestic plants at 70% capacity due to LNG cuts. Quietly approaching China. Kharif 2026 planting season at risk. India March CPI 3.40% with imported inflation at 6.49% (43% of headline).

Day 52. Sunday SNSC re-imposition of $1/bbl fees + Ras Laffan 3-5yr full recovery + North-only partial restart within one month = Friday CF -9.6% is Monday bounce setup. One-third of seaborne urea traverses Hormuz. Physical repair takes 6-18 months post-resolution regardless of deal rhetoric. Confirmed-action weight shift +2.0 on SNSC.

trigger_points.condition: Urea price sustained above $800/mt for 4+ weeks
trigger_points.mechanism: Would strain India's fertilizer subsidy budget. Either government absorbs (fiscal deficit) or passes to farmers (food inflation). Political sensitivity high ahead of state elections. Spot touched $838 on April 12 but futures at $693.50 -- watching whether spot sustains.

trigger_points.condition: India urea stocks fall below 40 LMT by May 1, 2026
trigger_points.mechanism: Would signal Kharif 2026 sowing area at serious risk of 10-15% reduction. Food inflation +5-8pp by Q4 FY27. Forces RBI to hold/hike rates instead of cutting. Political crisis for government ahead of state elections.
